title: SkillLearnBench: Benchmarking Continual Learning Methods for Agent Skill Generation on Real-World Tasks
authors: Shanshan Zhong, Yi Lu, Jingjie Ning, Yibing Wan, Lihan Feng, Yuyi Ao, Leonardo F. R. Ribeiro, Markus Dreyer, Sean Ammirati, Chenyan Xiong
abstract: Skills have become the de facto way to enable LLM agents to perform complex real-world tasks with customized instructions, workflows, and tools, but how to learn them automatically and effectively remains unclear. We introduce SkillLearnBench, the first benchmark for evaluating continual skill learning methods, comprising 20 verified, skill-dependent tasks across 15 sub-domains derived from a real-world skill taxonomy , evaluated at three levels: skill quality, execution trajectory, and task outcome. Using this benchmark, we evaluate recent continual learning techniques, those leveraging one-shot, self/teacher feedback, and skill creator to generate skills from agent experiences. We find that all continual learning methods improve over the no-skill baseline, yet consistent gains remain elusive: no method leads across all tasks and LLMs, and scaling to stronger LLMs does not reliably help. Continual learning improves tasks with clear, reusable workflows but struggles on open-ended tasks, and using stronger LLM backbones does not consistently produce better skills. Our analysis also revealed that multiple iterations in continual learning facilitate genuine improvement via external feedback, whereas self-feedback alone induces recursive drift. Our data and code are open-source at https://github.com/cxcscmu/SkillLearnBench to enable further studies of automatic skill generation and continual learning techniques.
title_zh: SkillLearnBench：面向真实世界任务的智能体技能生成持续学习方法基准测试
abstract_zh:
技能已成为实现大型语言模型（LLM）智能体完成复杂现实任务的默认方式，通过自定义指令、工作流程和工具来提升能力。然而，如何自动且高效地学习这些技能仍不明确。为此，我们提出了 SkillLearnBench——首个用于评估持续技能学习方法的基准测试，包含从真实世界技能分类体系中提炼出的15个子领域中的20个经过验证的、依赖于特定技能的任务，并在三个层面进行评估：技能质量、执行轨迹以及任务结果。

利用该基准，我们对近期的持续学习技术进行了评估，这些技术通过一次示例（one-shot）、自我反馈或教师反馈，以及技能生成器，从智能体的经验中自动构建技能。研究发现，所有持续学习方法均优于无技能基线，但稳定提升仍难以实现：没有任何一种方法在所有任务和LLM上表现领先，且提升更强的LLM也并不能可靠地带来性能改善。持续学习在具有清晰可复用工作流的任务中表现良好，但在开放性任务中则面临挑战；使用更强大的LLM骨干网络也并未始终产生更优的技能。

我们的分析还表明，持续学习中多次迭代结合外部反馈能够真正促进性能提升，而仅依赖自我反馈则容易引发递归偏差（recursive drift）。我们已将相关数据与代码开源至 https://github.com/cxcscmu/SkillLearnBench，以支持未来在自动技能生成与持续学习技术方面的深入研究。

title: Characterizing and Fixing Silent Data Loss in Spark-on-AWS-Lambda with Open Table Formats
authors: Srujan Kumar Gandla
abstract: AWS Lambda terminates containers with an uncatchable SIGKILL signal when a function exceeds its configured timeout. When a Spark-on-AWS-Lambda (SoAL) job is killed between Phase 1 (data upload) and Phase 2 (metadata commit) of a write, the result is silent data loss: orphaned Parquet files accumulate on S3 while the table's committed state remains unchanged and standard monitoring raises no alert. We characterize this vulnerability across Delta Lake and Apache Iceberg through 860 controlled kill-injection experiments at three dataset sizes. A SIGKILL landing in the inter-phase gap produced silent data loss in 100% of trials for both formats. We then present SafeWriter, a language-level wrapper that arms a watchdog thread 30 seconds before the Lambda timeout, triggers a format-native rollback via SQL, and records a checkpoint document on S3. SafeWriter converted every tested kill scenario into a clean, detectable rollback with under 100 ms added to normal write paths.
title_zh: 使用开放表格式在 Spark-on-AWS Lambda 中识别并修复静默数据丢失问题
abstract_zh: 当 AWS Lambda 函数超过其配置的超时时间时，系统会以无法捕获的 SIGKILL 信号终止容器。当 Spark-on-AWS-Lambda（SoAL）作业在写入操作的 Phase 1（数据上传）与 Phase 2（元数据提交）之间被终止时，会导致无声的数据丢失：S3 上积累大量孤立的 Parquet 文件，而表的已提交状态保持不变，标准监控也未发出任何警报。我们通过在三种不同数据集规模下进行的 860 次受控杀戮注入实验，对 Delta Lake 和 Apache Iceberg 这两种格式的该漏洞进行了全面分析。结果显示，在两个格式中，SIGKILL 信号恰好落在两阶段之间的间隙时，所有测试均导致了 100% 的无声数据丢失。随后，我们提出了 SafeWriter——一种语言级封装工具，它在 Lambda 超时前 30 秒启动一个守护线程，通过 SQL 语句触发格式原生的回滚，并在 S3 上记录一个检查点文档。SafeWriter 将所有测试的杀戮场景均转化为干净、可检测的回滚操作，且对正常写入路径的额外延迟不足 100 毫秒。

title: Efficient Test-Time Inference via Deterministic Exploration of Truncated Decoding Trees
authors: Xueyan Li, Johannes Zenn, Ekaterina Fadeeva, Guinan Su, Mrinmaya Sachan, Jonas Geiping
abstract: Self-consistency boosts inference-time performance by sampling multiple reasoning traces in parallel and voting. However, in constrained domains like math and code, this strategy is compute-inefficient because it samples with replacement, repeatedly revisiting the same high-probability prefixes and duplicate completions. We propose Distinct Leaf Enumeration (DLE), a deterministic decoding method that treats truncated sampling as traversal of a pruned decoding tree and systematically enumerates distinct leaves instead of sampling with replacement. This strategy improves inference efficiency in two ways. Algorithmically, it increases coverage of the truncated search space under a fixed budget by exploring previously unvisited high-probability branches. Systemically, it reuses shared prefixes and reduces redundant token generation. Empirically, DLE explores higher-quality reasoning traces than stochastic self-consistency, yielding better performance on math, coding, and general reasoning tasks.
title_zh: 通过截断解码树的确定性探索实现高效的测试时推理
abstract_zh: 自一致性通过并行采样多个推理路径并在结果上进行投票，提升了推理阶段的性能。然而，在数学和代码等受限领域，这种策略计算效率低下，因为其采用有放回采样，反复访问相同的高概率前缀和重复的完成内容。我们提出了一种确定性解码方法——**不同叶节点枚举（Distinct Leaf Enumeration, DLE）**，将截断采样视为对剪枝后解码树的遍历，并系统地枚举不同的叶节点，而非进行有放回采样。该策略在两个方面提升了推理效率：算法层面，它在固定预算下通过探索此前未访问过的高概率分支，扩大了截断搜索空间的覆盖范围；系统层面，它复用共享前缀，减少了冗余的词元生成。实验表明，DLE 比随机自一致性探索出更高质量的推理路径，在数学、编程及通用推理任务中均取得了更优的表现。

title: Mythos and the Unverified Cage: Z3-Based Pre-Deployment Verification for Frontier-Model Sandbox Infrastructure
authors: Dominik Blain
abstract: The April 2026 Claude Mythos sandbox escape exposed a critical weakness in frontier AI containment: the infrastructure surrounding advanced models remains susceptible to formally characterizable arithmetic vulnerabilities. Anthropic has not publicly characterized the escape vector; some secondary accounts hypothesize a CWE-190 arithmetic vulnerability in sandbox networking code. We treat this as unverified and analyze the vulnerability class rather than the specific escape. This paper presents COBALT, a Z3 SMT-based formal verification engine for identifying CWE-190/191/195 arithmetic vulnerability patterns in C/C++ infrastructure prior to deployment. We distinguish two classes of contribution. Validated: COBALT detects arithmetic vulnerability patterns in production codebases, producing SAT verdicts with concrete witnesses and UNSAT guarantees under explicit safety bounds. We demonstrate this on four production case studies: NASA cFE, wolfSSL, Eclipse Mosquitto, and NASA F Prime, with reproducible encodings, verified solver output, and acknowledged security outcomes. Proposed: a four-layer containment framework consisting of COBALT, VERDICT, DIRECTIVE-4, and SENTINEL, mapping pre-deployment verification, pre-execution constraints, output control, and runtime monitoring to the failure modes exposed by the Mythos incident. Under explicit assumptions, we further argue that the publicly reported Mythos escape class is consistent with a Z3-expressible CWE-190 arithmetic formulation and that pre-deployment formal analysis would have been capable of surfacing the relevant pattern. The broader claim is infrastructural: frontier-model safety cannot depend on behavioral safeguards alone; the containment stack itself must be subjected to formal verification.
title_zh: 神话与未经验证的牢笼：基于Z3的前沿模型沙箱基础设施预部署验证
abstract_zh:
2026年4月的“克劳德神话”沙盒逃逸事件暴露了前沿人工智能系统在隔离机制上的一个关键弱点：先进模型所依赖的基础设施仍容易受到可形式化描述的算术漏洞攻击。Anthropic公司尚未公开披露此次逃逸的具体路径；部分第三方分析推测，问题可能源于沙盒网络代码中的CWE-190（整数溢出）类漏洞。本文将该推测视为未经证实的假设，转而聚焦于对漏洞类别本身的分析，而非具体逃逸方式。

本文提出COBALT——一种基于Z3 SMT求解器的正式验证引擎，用于在部署前识别C/C++基础设施中CWE-190、CWE-191及CWE-195等算术漏洞模式。我们从两个层面贡献本研究：

**已验证成果**：COBALT能够在真实生产代码库中检测出算术漏洞模式，生成SAT（满足）结论并提供具体反例，或在明确的安全边界下给出UNSAT（不满足）的逻辑保证。我们在四个实际案例中验证了该方法的有效性：NASA cFE、wolfSSL、Eclipse Mosquitto以及NASA F Prime。所有实验均提供可复现的编码实现、经验证的求解器输出，并获得相关安全团队的确认反馈。

**提出的新框架**：我们设计了一个四层防护体系——COBALT、VERDICT、DIRECTIVE-4与SENTINEL，分别对应部署前的形式化验证、执行前约束控制、输出内容管控以及运行时监控，以应对“神话”事件中暴露出的各类失效模式。在明确假设前提下，我们进一步论证：公众报告的“神话”逃逸模式，完全可被形式化为Z3可表达的CWE-190算术模型；若在部署前进行形式化分析，此类漏洞模式本应能够被提前发现。

更广泛而言，本文主张：前沿大模型的安全保障不能仅依赖行为层面的防护措施；整个隔离架构本身也必须接受形式化验证。唯有如此，才能真正构建可信、可验证的AI安全防线。

title: Extending Contract Verification for Parallel Programming Models to Fortran
authors: Yussur Mustafa Oraji, Christian Bischof
abstract: High-performance computing often relies on parallel programming models such as MPI for distributed-memory systems. While powerful, these models are prone to subtle programming errors, leading to development of multiple correctness checking tools. However, these are often limited to C/C++ codes, tied to specific library implementations, or restricted to certain error classes. Building on our prior work with CoVer, a generic, contract-based verification framework for parallel programming models, we extend CoVer's applicability to Fortran, enabling static and dynamic analysis across multiple programming languages. We adapted language-specific contract definitions and modified the analyses to support both C/C++ and Fortran programs. Our evaluation demonstrates that the enhanced version preserves CoVer's analysis accuracy and even revealed a bug in the MPI-BugBench testing framework, underscoring the effectiveness of the approach. The Fortran port of CoVer turns out to be substantially more efficient than the state-of-the-art tool MUST, while maintaining generality across languages.
title_zh: 将合同验证扩展到并行编程模型的Fortran语言
abstract_zh: 高性能计算通常依赖于MPI等并行编程模型来处理分布式内存系统。尽管这些模型功能强大，但容易产生微妙的编程错误，因此催生了多种正确性检查工具的发展。然而，这些工具往往局限于C/C++代码，与特定库实现绑定，或仅针对某些类型的错误。基于我们之前在CoVer方面的研究——一种通用的、基于契约的并行编程模型验证框架——我们进一步拓展了CoVer的应用范围至Fortran语言，实现了对多种编程语言的静态和动态分析。我们调整了针对不同语言的契约定义，并修改了分析机制，使其同时支持C/C++和Fortran程序。评估结果表明，改进后的版本保持了CoVer原有的分析准确性，甚至发现了一个MPI-BugBench测试框架中的缺陷，充分证明了该方法的有效性。此外，CoVer的Fortran版本在性能上显著优于当前最先进的工具MUST，同时仍保持跨语言的通用性。

title: Towards Secure Logging: Characterizing and Benchmarking Logging Code Security Issues with LLMs
authors: He Yang Yuan, Xin Wang, Kundi Yao, An Ran Chen, Zishuo Ding, Zhenhao Li
abstract: Logging code plays an important role in software systems by recording key events and behaviors, which are essential for debugging and monitoring. However, insecure logging practices can inadvertently expose sensitive information or enable attacks such as log injection, posing serious threats to system security and privacy. Prior research has examined general defects in logging code, but systematic analysis of logging code security issues remains limited, particularly in leveraging LLMs for detection and repair. In this paper, we derive a comprehensive taxonomy of logging code security issues, encompassing four common issue categories and 10 corresponding patterns. We further construct a benchmark dataset with 101 real-world logging security issue reports that have been manually reviewed and annotated. We then propose an automated framework that incorporates various contextual knowledge to evaluate LLMs' capabilities in detecting and repairing logging security issues. Our experimental results reveal a notable disparity in performance: while LLMs are moderately effective at detecting security issues (e.g., the accuracy ranges from 12.9% to 52.5% on average), they face noticeable challenges in reliably generating correct code repairs. We also find that the issue description alone improves the LLMs' detection accuracy more than the security pattern explanation or a combination of both. Overall, our findings provide actionable insights for practitioners and highlight the potential and limitations of current LLMs for secure logging.
title_zh: 面向安全日志记录：利用大语言模型对日志代码安全问题进行特征分析与基准测试
abstract_zh: 日志代码在软件系统中扮演着重要角色，通过记录关键事件和行为，为调试和监控提供支持。然而，不安全的日志编写实践可能无意中泄露敏感信息，或导致诸如日志注入等攻击，对系统安全与隐私构成严重威胁。以往研究虽已关注日志代码中的通用缺陷，但针对日志代码安全问题的系统性分析仍较为有限，尤其是在利用大语言模型（LLMs）进行检测与修复方面。本文提出一个全面的日志代码安全问题分类体系，涵盖四大常见问题类别及10种具体模式。我们进一步构建了一个基准数据集，包含101个经过人工审核与标注的真实世界日志安全问题报告。在此基础上，我们设计了一套自动化框架，融合多种上下文知识，评估LLMs在检测与修复日志安全问题方面的表现。实验结果表明，LLMs在检测安全问题方面表现中等（例如，平均准确率在12.9%至52.5%之间），但在生成正确修复代码方面仍面临显著挑战。此外，我们发现仅依赖问题描述即可比仅使用安全模式解释，或两者结合，更有效地提升LLMs的检测准确率。总体而言，本研究为实践者提供了可操作的洞见，揭示了当前LLMs在安全日志开发中的潜力与局限性。

title: Parallel-SFT: Improving Zero-Shot Cross-Programming-Language Transfer for Code RL
authors: Zhaofeng Wu, Shiqi Wang, Boya Peng, Anuj Goyal, Melanie Kambadur, Sebastian Ruder, Yoon Kim, Chloe Bi
abstract: Modern language models demonstrate impressive coding capabilities in common programming languages (PLs), such as C++ and Python, but their performance in lower-resource PLs is often limited by training data availability. In principle, however, most programming skills are universal across PLs, so the capability acquired in one PL should transfer to others. In this work, we propose the task of zero-shot cross-programming-language transfer for code RL. We find that, for Llama-3.1, RL training for code generation in a source PL fails to improve, and sometimes even degrades, the performance on other target PLs. To address this, we hypothesize that effective RL transfer requires a generalizable SFT initialization before RL. We thus propose **Parallel-SFT**, an SFT strategy that incorporates "parallel programs" -- functionally equivalent code implemented in multiple PLs -- into the data mixture. We demonstrate that this improves transferability: when we subsequently perform RL on our Parallel-SFT model, we observe better generalization to unseen PLs. Analysis of the model internal representations reveals that Parallel-SFT leads to a more functionality-centric latent space, where equivalent programs across PLs are more tightly clustered, which we hypothesize to contribute to the improved transferability.
title_zh: 并行SFT：提升代码强化学习中的零样本跨编程语言迁移能力
abstract_zh: 现代语言模型在常见编程语言（PLs）如 C++ 和 Python 中展现出令人印象深刻的编码能力，但在低资源编程语言上的表现往往受限于训练数据的匮乏。然而，从原则上讲，大多数编程技能在不同编程语言间具有普适性，因此在一种语言中习得的能力应能迁移到其他语言。本文提出了一种面向代码强化学习（RL）的零样本跨编程语言迁移任务。我们发现，对于 Llama-3.1 来说，在源语言上进行代码生成的 RL 训练不仅未能提升其他目标语言的表现，有时甚至导致性能下降。为解决这一问题，我们假设有效的 RL 迁移需要在 RL 之前具备可泛化的 SFT（监督微调）初始化。为此，我们提出了 **Parallel-SFT**——一种将“并行程序”（即功能等价但在多种编程语言中实现的代码）纳入数据混合中的 SFT 策略。实验表明，该方法显著提升了模型的迁移能力：在 Parallel-SFT 模型基础上进行后续 RL 训练时，模型对未见过的编程语言表现出更强的泛化能力。对模型内部表示的分析显示，Parallel-SFT 促使模型的潜在空间更具功能性特征，使得跨语言的功能等价程序在表征空间中聚类更紧密，我们推测这正是其迁移能力提升的关键原因。

title: R2IF: Aligning Reasoning with Decisions via Composite Rewards for Interpretable LLM Function Calling
authors: Aijia Cheng, Kailong Wang, Ling Shi, Yongxin Zhao
abstract: Function calling empowers large language models (LLMs) to interface with external tools, yet existing RL-based approaches suffer from misalignment between reasoning processes and tool-call decisions. We propose R2IF, a reasoning-aware RL framework for interpretable function calling, adopting a composite reward integrating format/correctness constraints, Chain-of-Thought Effectiveness Reward (CER), and Specification-Modification-Value (SMV) reward, optimized via GRPO. Experiments on BFCL/ACEBench show R2IF outperforms baselines by up to 34.62% (Llama3.2-3B on BFCL) with positive Average CoT Effectiveness (0.05 for Llama3.2-3B), enhancing both function-calling accuracy and interpretability for reliable tool-augmented LLM deployment.
title_zh: R2IF：通过复合奖励实现推理与决策对齐以实现可解释的大语言模型函数调用
abstract_zh: 函数调用使大型语言模型（LLMs）能够与外部工具交互，但现有的基于强化学习（RL）的方法在推理过程与工具调用决策之间存在不一致问题。我们提出R2IF，一种面向可解释函数调用的、具备推理感知能力的强化学习框架，采用融合格式/正确性约束、思维链有效性奖励（CER）以及规范-修改-价值（SMV）奖励的复合奖励机制，并通过GRPO进行优化。在BFCL/ACEBench上的实验表明，R2IF相较于基线方法性能提升最高达34.62%（Llama3.2-3B在BFCL上），同时保持正向平均思维链有效性（Llama3.2-3B为0.05），显著提升了函数调用的准确率与可解释性，为可靠工具增强型LLM部署提供了有力支持。

title: Self-Aware Vector Embeddings for Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Neuroscience-Inspired Framework for Temporal, Confidence-Weighted, and Relational Knowledge
authors: Naizhong Xu
abstract: Modern retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems treat vector embeddings as static, context-free artifacts: an embedding has no notion of when it was created, how trustworthy its source is, or which other embeddings depend on it. This flattening of knowledge has a measurable cost: recent work on VersionRAG reports that conventional RAG achieves only 58% accuracy on versioned technical queries, because retrieval returns semantically similar but temporally invalid content. We propose SmartVector, a framework that augments dense embeddings with three explicit properties -- temporal awareness, confidence decay, and relational awareness -- and a five-stage lifecycle modeled on hippocampal-neocortical memory consolidation. A retrieval pipeline replaces pure cosine similarity with a four-signal score that mixes semantic relevance, temporal validity, live confidence, and graph-relational importance. A background consolidation agent detects contradictions, builds dependency edges, and propagates updates along those edges as graph-neural-network-style messages. Confidence is governed by a closed-form function combining an Ebbinghaus-style exponential decay, user-feedback reconsolidation, and logarithmic access reinforcement. We formalize the model, relate it to temporal knowledge graph embedding, agentic memory architectures, and uncertainty-aware RAG, and present a reference implementation. On a reproducible synthetic versioned-policy benchmark of 258 vectors and 138 queries, SmartVector roughly doubles top-1 accuracy over plain cosine RAG (62.0% vs. 31.0% on a held-out split), drops stale-answer rate from 35.0% to 13.3%, cuts Expected Calibration Error by nearly 2x (0.244 vs. 0.470), reduces re-embedding cost per single-word edit by 77%, and is robust across contradiction-injection rates from 0% to 75%.
title_zh: 用于检索增强生成的自感知向量嵌入：一种受神经科学启发的时序性、置信度加权及关系型知识框架
abstract_zh: 现代检索增强生成（RAG）系统将向量嵌入视为静态、与上下文无关的实体：一个嵌入并不具备创建时间、来源可信度或与其他嵌入依赖关系的概念。这种知识扁平化带来了可度量的代价：近期关于VersionRAG的研究表明，传统RAG在版本化技术查询上的准确率仅为58%，因为检索返回的内容虽然语义相似，但在时间上已过时。我们提出SmartVector框架，通过为密集嵌入显式引入三种属性——时间感知、置信度衰减和关系感知——并采用受海马体-新皮层记忆巩固机制启发的五阶段生命周期模型，来解决这一问题。检索管道将纯余弦相似度替换为一种四信号评分机制，综合考虑语义相关性、时间有效性、实时置信度以及图结构中的关系重要性。后台整合代理负责检测矛盾、构建依赖边，并以类似图神经网络消息传递的方式沿这些边传播更新。置信度由一个闭式函数控制，该函数结合了埃宾浩斯风格的指数衰减、用户反馈驱动的再巩固机制以及对数形式的访问强化。我们形式化了该模型，将其与时间知识图谱嵌入、智能体记忆架构及不确定性感知RAG进行了关联，并提供了参考实现。在一个包含258个向量和138个查询的可复现合成版本化策略基准测试中，SmartVector相较于单纯的余弦相似度RAG，将Top-1准确率大致翻倍（在保留测试集上分别为62.0% vs. 31.0%），将过时答案率从35.0%降至13.3%，使期望校准误差几乎减半（0.244 vs. 0.470），单字编辑的重新嵌入成本降低77%，且在矛盾注入率从0%到75%的范围内均表现出强鲁棒性。

title: Automatic Code and Test Generation of Smart Contracts from Coordination Models
authors: Elvis Konjoh Selabi, Maurizio Murgia, António Ravara, Emilio Tuosto
abstract: We propose a formal approach for specifying and implementing decentralised coordination in distributed systems, with a focus on smart contracts. Our model captures dynamic roles, data-driven transitions, and external coordination interfaces, enabling high-level reasoning about decentralised workflows. We implement a toolchain that supports formal model validation, code generation for Solidity (our framework is extendable to other smart contract languages), and automated test synthesis. Although our implementation targets blockchain platforms, the methodology is platform-agnostic and may generalise to other service-oriented and distributed architectures. We demonstrate the expressiveness and practicality of the approach by modelling and realising some coordination patterns in smart contracts.
title_zh: 从协调模型自动生成智能合约及其测试用例
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种形式化方法，用于指定和实现分布式系统中的去中心化协调，特别关注智能合约。我们的模型捕捉了动态角色、数据驱动的转换以及外部协调接口，从而支持对去中心化工作流进行高层次的推理。我们实现了一个工具链，支持形式化模型验证、Solidity代码生成（我们的框架可扩展至其他智能合约语言），以及自动化测试生成。尽管我们的实现针对区块链平台，但该方法具有平台无关性，可推广至其他面向服务和分布式架构。通过在智能合约中建模并实现一些协调模式，我们展示了该方法的表达能力和实际应用价值。

title: Finding Duplicates in 1.1M BDD Steps: cukereuse, a Paraphrase-Robust Static Detector for Cucumber and Gherkin
authors: Ali Hassaan Mughal, Noor Fatima, Muhammad Bilal
abstract: Behaviour-Driven Development (BDD) suites accumulate step-text duplication whose maintenance cost is established in prior work. Existing detection techniques require running the tests (Binamungu et al., 2018-2023) or are confined to a single organisation (Irshad et al., 2020-2022), leaving a gap: a purely static, paraphrase-robust, step-level detector usable on any repository. We fill the gap with cukereuse, an open-source Python CLI combining exact hashing, Levenshtein ratio, and sentence-transformer embeddings in a layered pipeline, released alongside an empirical corpus of 347 public GitHub repositories, 23,667 parsed .feature files, and 1,113,616 Gherkin steps. The step-weighted exact-duplicate rate is 80.2 %; the median-repository rate is 58.6 % (Spearman rho = 0.51 with size). The top hybrid cluster groups 20.7k occurrences across 2.2k files. Against 1,020 pairs manually labelled by the three authors under a released rubric (inter-annotator Fleiss' kappa = 0.84 on a 60-pair overlap), we report precision, recall, and F1 with bootstrap 95 % CIs under two protocols: the primary rubric and a score-free second-pass relabelling. The strongest honest pair-level number is near-exact at F1 = 0.822 on score-free labels; the primary-rubric semantic F1 = 0.906 is inflated by a stratification artefact that pins recall at 1.000. Lexical baselines (SourcererCC-style, NiCad-style) reach primary F1 = 0.761 and 0.799. The paper also presents a CDN-structured critique of Gherkin (Cognitive Dimensions of Notations); eight of fourteen dimensions are rated problematic or unsupported. The tool, corpus, labelled pairs, rubric, and pipeline are released under permissive licences.
title_zh: 在110万条BDD步骤中查找重复项：cukereuse，一种针对Cucumber和Gherkin的抗改写静态检测工具
abstract_zh:
行为驱动开发（BDD）测试套件中积累了大量步骤文本重复，其维护成本已在前期研究中得到证实。现有检测技术要么需要运行测试（Binamungu等，2018–2023），要么局限于单一组织内部使用（Irshad等，2020–2022），因而存在一个空白：亟需一种纯粹静态、对改写具有鲁棒性、可在任意代码仓库上使用的步骤级重复检测工具。本文填补了这一空白，提出了 cukereuse——一款开源的Python命令行工具，采用分层流水线设计，结合精确哈希、Levenshtein相似度比率以及句子嵌入（sentence-transformer）表示方法。同时，我们发布了包含347个公开GitHub仓库、23,667个解析后的.feature文件和1,113,616个Gherkin步骤的实证数据集。

在该数据集中，步骤加权的完全重复率高达80.2%；各仓库的中位重复率为58.6%（与仓库规模之间的Spearman等级相关系数ρ = 0.51）。表现最强的混合聚类组共涵盖20,700次重复出现，分布在2,200个文件中。针对三位作者依据已发布的评估标准手动标注的1,020对样本（在60对重叠样本上的组间一致性Fleiss' kappa = 0.84），我们在两种协议下报告了置信区间为95%的自举法（bootstrap）计算出的精确率、召回率和F1分数：主评估标准和无评分的第二轮重新标注。在无评分标签下的最佳诚实配对级别结果接近完全一致，F1达到0.822；而基于主评估标准的语义F1为0.906，但因分层偏差导致召回率被固定在1.000，存在高估现象。作为对比，基于词法的基线方法（SourcererCC风格与NiCad风格）在主评估标准下的F1分别为0.761和0.799。

此外，本文还基于“记号的认知维度”（Cognitive Dimensions of Notations, CDN）框架对Gherkin语言进行了结构化批判分析，其中十四项维度中有八项被评定为存在问题或缺乏支持。本研究提供的工具、数据集、标注对、评估标准及处理流程均已采用宽松许可协议开源发布。

title: Hallucination Inspector: A Fact-Checking Judge for API Migration
authors: Marcos Tileria, Santanu Kumar Dash, Profir-Petru Pârţachi, Earl T. Barr
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in automated software engineering for tasks such as API migration. While LLMs are able to identify migration patterns, they often make mistakes and fail to produce correct glue code to invoke the new API in place of the old one. We call this issue Scaffolding Hallucination, a failure mode where models generate incorrect calling contexts by inventing Phantom Symbols -- such as imaginary imports, constructors, and constants -- that do not exist in the API specification. In this paper, we show that standard metrics cannot be relied upon to detect these instances of hallucination. We propose Hallucination Inspector, a static analysis tool to detect Scaffolding Hallucination in LLM-generated code. Our approach includes a lightweight evaluation framework that verifies symbols extracted from the abstract syntax tree against a knowledge base derived directly from software documentation for the API. A preliminary evaluation on Android API migrations demonstrates that our approach successfully identifies hallucinations and significantly reduces false positives compared to standard metrics and probabilistic judges
title_zh: 幻觉检查员：API 迁移的事实核查裁判
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在自动化软件工程中的应用日益广泛，例如用于API迁移任务。尽管LLMs能够识别迁移模式，但它们常常出错，无法正确生成调用新API替代旧API所需的“粘合代码”（glue code）。我们称这一问题为“脚手架幻觉”（Scaffolding Hallucination），即模型通过虚构不存在于API规范中的“幽灵符号”——如虚构的导入语句、构造函数和常量——来生成错误的调用上下文。本文表明，传统的评估指标无法可靠地检测此类幻觉现象。为此，我们提出了“幻觉检查器”（Hallucination Inspector），一种静态分析工具，用于检测LLM生成代码中的脚手架幻觉。我们的方法包含一个轻量级评估框架，该框架将从抽象语法树中提取的符号与直接源自API软件文档的知识库进行比对验证。针对Android API迁移的初步评估表明，该方法能够有效识别幻觉现象，并显著降低误报率，相较于传统指标和概率判断器具有明显优势。

title: Diagnosing CFG Interpretation in LLMs
authors: Hanqi Li, Lu Chen, Kai Yu
abstract: As LLMs are increasingly integrated into agentic systems, they must adhere to dynamically defined, machine-interpretable interfaces. We evaluate LLMs as in-context interpreters: given a novel context-free grammar, can LLMs generate syntactically valid, behaviorally functional, and semantically faithful outputs? We introduce RoboGrid, a framework that disentangles syntax, behavior, and semantics through controlled stress-tests of recursion depth, expression complexity, and surface styles. Our experiments reveal a consistent hierarchical degradation: LLMs often maintain surface syntax but fail to preserve structural semantics. Despite the partial mitigation provided by CoT reasoning, performance collapses under structural density, specifically deep recursion and high branching, with semantic alignment vanishing at extreme depths. Furthermore, "Alien" lexicons reveal that LLMs rely on semantic bootstrapping from keywords rather than pure symbolic induction. These findings pinpoint critical gaps in hierarchical state-tracking required for reliable, grammar-agnostic agents.
title_zh: 大语言模型中CFG解释的诊断
abstract_zh: 随着大型语言模型（LLMs）越来越多地融入智能体系统，它们必须遵循动态定义且机器可解析的接口规范。本文评估了LLMs作为上下文内解释器的能力：在给定一个全新的无上下文语法（context-free grammar）时，LLMs能否生成语法正确、行为有效且语义忠实的输出？为此，我们提出了RoboGrid框架，通过控制递归深度、表达式复杂度和表面风格等维度的应力测试，将语法、行为与语义三者解耦。实验结果揭示出一种一致的层级退化现象：LLMs通常能保持表面语法结构，但在深层结构语义上却难以维持一致性。尽管思维链（CoT）推理在一定程度上缓解了这一问题，但当面对高结构密度场景——特别是深层递归与高分支结构时，性能急剧下降，语义对齐能力在极端深度下完全消失。此外，“外星”词汇（Alien lexicons）的实验表明，LLMs并非依赖纯粹的符号归纳，而是依靠关键词进行语义“自举”（semantic bootstrapping）。这些发现指出了当前LLMs在实现可靠、语法无关智能体时所面临的关键缺陷：即缺乏对层次化状态的有效追踪能力。

title: Autonomous LLM-generated Feedback for Student Exercises in Introductory Software Engineering Courses
authors: Andreas Metzger
abstract: Introductory Software Engineering (SE) courses face rapidly increasing student enrollment numbers, participants with diverse backgrounds and the influence of Generative AI (GenAI) solutions. High teacher-to-student ratios often challenge providing timely, high-quality, and personalized feedback a significant challenge for educators. To address these challenges, we introduce NAILA, a tool that provides 24/7 autonomous feedback for student exercises. Utilizing GenAI in the form of modern LLMs, NAILA processes student solutions provided in open document formats, evaluating them against teacher-defined model solutions through specialized prompt templates. We conducted an empirical study involving 900+ active students at the University of Duisburg-Essen to assess four main research questions investigating (1) the underlying motivations that drive students to either adopt or reject NAILA, (2) user acceptance by measuring perceived usefulness and ease of use alongside subjective learning progress, (3) how often and how consistently students engage with NAILA, and (4) how using NAILA to receive AI feedback impacts on academic performance compared to human feedback.
title_zh: 自主生成的大型语言模型反馈用于软件工程导论课程的学生练习
abstract_zh: 软件工程（SE）入门课程面临着学生人数迅速增长、学习者背景多样以及生成式人工智能（GenAI）技术影响等多重挑战。师生比例过高，使得教师难以及时、高质量且个性化地提供反馈，成为教育工作者的一大难题。为应对这些挑战，我们提出了NAILA——一款可实现全天候自主反馈的学生练习辅助工具。NAILA利用现代大语言模型（LLM）作为生成式人工智能核心，能够处理以开放文档格式提交的学生解答，并通过教师预先设定的参考答案，借助专门设计的提示模板进行评估。我们在杜伊斯堡-埃森大学开展了一项实证研究，涵盖900多名活跃学生，旨在探讨四个核心研究问题：（1）驱动学生选择使用或拒绝NAILA的内在动因；（2）用户接受度，通过感知有用性、易用性以及主观学习进展等维度进行测量；（3）学生使用NAILA的频率与持续性；（4）相较于人工反馈，使用NAILA获取AI反馈对学生学业表现的影响。

title: Synthesizing Multi-Agent Harnesses for Vulnerability Discovery
authors: Hanzhi Liu, Chaofan Shou, Xiaonan Liu, Hongbo Wen, Yanju Chen, Ryan Jingyang Fang, Yu Feng
abstract: LLM agents have begun to find real security vulnerabilities that human auditors and automated fuzzers missed for decades, in source-available targets where the analyst can build and instrument the code. In practice the work is split among several agents, wired together by a harness: the program that fixes which roles exist, how they pass information, which tools each may call, and how retries are coordinated. When the language model is held fixed, changing only the harness can still change success rates by several-fold on public agent benchmarks, yet most harnesses are written by hand; recent harness optimizers each search only a narrow slice of the design space and rely on coarse pass/fail feedback that gives no diagnostic signal about why a trial failed. AgentFlow addresses both limitations with a typed graph DSL whose search space jointly covers agent roles, prompts, tools, communication topology, and coordination protocol, paired with a feedback-driven outer loop that reads runtime signals from the target program itself to diagnose which part of the harness caused the failure and rewrite it accordingly. We evaluate AgentFlow on TerminalBench-2 with Claude Opus 4.6 and on Google Chrome with Kimi K2.5. AgentFlow reaches 84.3% on TerminalBench-2, the highest score in the public leaderboard snapshot we evaluate against, and discovers ten previously unknown zero-day vulnerabilities in Google Chrome, including two Critical sandbox-escape vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-5280 and CVE-2026-6297).
title_zh: 用于漏洞发现的多智能体测试框架的构建
abstract_zh:
大型语言模型（LLM）代理已开始发现人类审计员和自动化模糊测试工具几十年来一直未能察觉的安全漏洞，这些漏洞存在于源码可获取的目标中，分析人员可以构建并注入探针代码。在实际应用中，这项工作由多个代理协同完成，通过一个“集成框架”进行连接：该程序定义了角色的设置、信息传递方式、各代理可调用的工具，以及重试机制的协调方式。当语言模型保持不变时，仅改变集成框架即可使公开代理基准测试中的成功率提升数倍。然而，目前大多数框架仍由人工编写；近期的框架优化器仅在设计空间的一个狭窄范围内搜索，并依赖粗粒度的成功/失败反馈，无法提供关于失败原因的诊断信息。

AgentFlow 通过引入一种类型化的图式领域特定语言（DSL），同时解决了上述两个局限。该 DSL 的搜索空间涵盖了代理角色、提示词、工具选择、通信拓扑结构以及协调协议等多个维度。同时，它还配备了一个基于运行时反馈的外部循环机制，能够读取目标程序自身的运行信号，从而诊断出导致失败的具体框架部分，并针对性地进行重构。我们在 TerminalBench-2 上使用 Claude Opus 4.6，在 Google Chrome 上使用 Kimi K2.5 对 AgentFlow 进行了评估。结果显示，AgentFlow 在 TerminalBench-2 上取得了 84.3% 的成绩，是我们在评估所参考的公开排行榜快照中最高的得分；此外，它还在 Google Chrome 中发现了十个此前未知的零日漏洞，其中包括两个严重的沙箱逃逸漏洞（CVE-2026-5280 和 CVE-2026-6297）。

title: CVEs With a CVSS Score Greater Than or Equal to 9
authors: Lena Sinterhauf, Andreas Aßmuth, Roland Kaltefleiter
abstract: Critical vulnerabilities with Common Vulnerability Scoring System scores of 9.0 or higher pose severe risks to organisations' information systems. Timely detection and remediation are essential to minimise economic and reputational damage from cyberattacks. This paper provides a thorough analysis of the identification and resolution timelines of such critical vulnerabilities. A mixed-methods approach is employed, integrating quantitative data from global vulnerability databases analysing 245,456 Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures records spanning from 2009 to 2024, of which 12.8 % were critical, with qualitative case studies of notable incidents. This methodical combination of quantitative and qualitative data sources enables the identification of patterns and delay factors in vulnerability management. The findings indicate significant delays in public disclosure and patch deployment, influenced by industry-specific factors, resource availability and organisational processes. The paper concludes with a series of actionable recommendations to improve the efficiency of vulnerability responses. Despite faster disclosure, the remediation gap for critical vulnerabilities remains a systemic risk, driven by organisational inertia and system complexity.
title_zh: CVSS评分大于或等于9的漏洞（CVE）
abstract_zh: CVSS评分达到9.0或更高的严重漏洞对组织的信息系统构成重大威胁。及时发现并修复这些漏洞对于减少网络攻击带来的经济和声誉损失至关重要。本文对这类关键漏洞的识别与修复时间线进行了全面分析。研究采用混合方法，结合来自全球漏洞数据库的定量数据——涵盖2009年至2024年间共计245,456条通用漏洞与暴露（CVE）记录，其中12.8%为关键级别——以及对重大事件的定性案例研究。这种定量与定性数据源的系统性整合，有助于识别漏洞管理中的模式及延迟因素。研究结果表明，公开披露和补丁部署存在显著延迟，其影响因素包括行业特性、资源可得性以及组织内部流程。文章最后提出一系列可操作的建议，以提升漏洞响应效率。尽管披露速度有所加快，但关键漏洞的修复滞后问题仍构成系统性风险，主要由组织惯性与系统复杂性所驱动。

title: Learning to Evolve: A Self-Improving Framework for Multi-Agent Systems via Textual Parameter Graph Optimization
authors: Shan He, Runze Wang, Zhuoyun Du, Huiyu Bai, Zouying Cao, Yu Cheng, Bo Zheng
abstract: Designing and optimizing multi-agent systems (MAS) is a complex, labor-intensive process of "Agent Engineering." Existing automatic optimization methods, primarily focused on flat prompt tuning, lack the structural awareness to debug the intricate web of interactions in MAS. More critically, these optimizers are static; they do not learn from experience to improve their own optimization strategies. To address these gaps, we introduce Textual Parameter Graph Optimization (TPGO), a framework that enables a multi-agent system to learn to evolve. TPGO first models the MAS as a Textual Parameter Graph (TPG), where agents, tools, and workflows are modular, optimizable nodes. To guide evolution, we derive "textual gradients," structured natural language feedback from execution traces, to pinpoint failures and suggest granular modifications. The core of our framework is Group Relative Agent Optimization (GRAO), a novel meta-learning strategy that learns from historical optimization experiences. By analyzing past successes and failures, GRAO becomes progressively better at proposing effective updates, allowing the system to learn how to optimize itself. Extensive experiments on complex benchmarks like GAIA and MCP-Universe show that TPGO significantly enhances the performance of state-of-the-art agent frameworks, achieving higher success rates through automated, self-improving optimization.
title_zh: 学习进化：通过文本参数图优化实现多智能体系统的自我改进框架
abstract_zh:
设计和优化多智能体系统（MAS）是一项复杂且耗时的“智能体工程”过程。现有的自动优化方法主要聚焦于扁平化的提示调优，缺乏对MAS中错综复杂的交互关系的结构化认知，难以有效调试。更关键的是，这些优化器是静态的，无法从经验中学习以改进自身的优化策略。为解决上述问题，我们提出了文本参数图优化（Textual Parameter Graph Optimization, TPGO）框架，使多智能体系统具备自我演化的学习能力。

TPGO首先将多智能体系统建模为一个**文本参数图**（Textual Parameter Graph, TPG），其中智能体、工具和工作流均作为模块化、可优化的节点。为了引导系统的演化，我们从执行轨迹中提取出“文本梯度”——一种结构化的自然语言反馈，能够精准定位失败原因，并提出细粒度的修改建议。该框架的核心是**组相对智能体优化**（Group Relative Agent Optimization, GRAO），一种新颖的元学习策略，能够基于历史优化经验进行学习。通过分析过往的成功与失败案例，GRAO逐步提升其提出有效更新方案的能力，从而使系统学会如何自我优化。

在GAIA和MCP-Universe等复杂基准测试上的大量实验表明，TPGO显著提升了现有顶尖智能体框架的性能，通过自动化、自进化的优化方式实现了更高的成功率。

title: Hybrid Policy Distillation for LLMs
authors: Wenhong Zhu, Ruobing Xie, Rui Wang, Pengfei Liu
abstract: Knowledge distillation (KD) is a powerful paradigm for compressing large language models (LLMs), whose effectiveness depends on intertwined choices of divergence direction, optimization strategy, and data regime. We break down the design of existing KD methods and present a unified view that establishes connections between them, reformulating KD as a reweighted log-likelihood objective at the token level. We further propose Hybrid Policy Distillation (HPD), which integrates the complementary advantages of forward and reverse KL to balance mode coverage and mode-seeking, and combines off-policy data with lightweight, approximate on-policy sampling. We validate HPD on long-generation math reasoning as well as short-generation dialogue and code tasks, demonstrating improved optimization stability, computational efficiency, and final performance across diverse model families and scales. The code related to this work is available at https://github.com/zwhong714/Hybrid-Policy-Distillation.
title_zh: 大语言模型的混合策略蒸馏
abstract_zh: 知识蒸馏（Knowledge Distillation, KD）是一种强大的大语言模型（LLM）压缩范式，其有效性取决于发散方向、优化策略与数据设置之间相互交织的选择。我们对现有KD方法的设计进行了分解，并提出一个统一视角，揭示了各类方法之间的内在联系，将知识蒸馏重新表述为在token层面的加权对数似然目标。在此基础上，我们提出了混合策略蒸馏（Hybrid Policy Distillation, HPD），该方法融合了前向KL与反向KL的优势，有效平衡了模式覆盖与模式聚焦，同时结合了离策略数据与轻量级、近似的同策略采样。我们在长序列生成的数学推理任务以及短文本生成的对话和代码生成任务上验证了HPD的有效性，结果表明，HPD在多种模型架构与规模下均展现出更优的优化稳定性、计算效率及最终性能。相关代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/zwhong714/Hybrid-Policy-Distillation。

title: Taint-Style Vulnerability Detection and Confirmation for Node.js Packages Using LLM Agent Reasoning
authors: Ronghao Ni, Mihai Christodorescu, Limin Jia
abstract: The rapidly evolving Node$.$js ecosystem currently includes millions of packages and is a critical part of modern software supply chains, making vulnerability detection of Node$.$js packages increasingly important. However, traditional program analysis struggles in this setting because of dynamic JavaScript features and the large number of package dependencies. Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) and the emerging paradigm of LLM-based agents offer an alternative to handcrafted program models. This raises the question of whether an LLM-centric, tool-augmented approach can effectively detect and confirm taint-style vulnerabilities (e.g., arbitrary command injection) in Node$.$js packages. We implement LLMVD$.$js, a multi-stage agent pipeline to scan code, propose vulnerabilities, generate proof-of-concept exploits, and validate them through lightweight execution oracles; and systematically evaluate its effectiveness in taint-style vulnerability detection and confirmation in Node$.$js packages without dedicated static/dynamic analysis engines for path derivation. For packages from public benchmarks, LLMVD$.$js confirms 84% of the vulnerabilities, compared to less than 22% for prior program analysis tools. It also outperforms a prior LLM-program-analysis hybrid approach while requiring neither vulnerability annotations nor prior vulnerability reports. When evaluated on a set of 260 recently released packages (without vulnerability groundtruth information), traditional tools produce validated exploits for few ($\leq 2$) packages, while LLMVD$.$js generates validated exploits for 36 packages.
title_zh: 使用大语言模型代理推理的 Node.js 包污点风格漏洞检测与验证
abstract_zh:
不断演进的 Node.js 生态系统目前包含数百万个包，已成为现代软件供应链中的关键组成部分，因此对 Node.js 包进行漏洞检测变得日益重要。然而，传统的程序分析方法在此场景下面临挑战，主要由于 JavaScript 的动态特性以及包依赖关系数量庞大。近年来，大型语言模型（LLMs）的发展以及基于 LLM 的智能体新范式为替代手工构建的程序模型提供了新的可能。这引出了一个核心问题：是否可以采用以 LLM 为中心、辅以工具增强的方法，有效检测并验证 Node.js 包中的污染型漏洞（如任意命令注入）？

我们实现了 LLMVD.js，这是一个多阶段智能体流水线，能够扫描代码、提出潜在漏洞、生成概念验证（PoC）攻击代码，并通过轻量级执行断言来验证这些漏洞；同时，我们在不依赖专用静态/动态分析引擎进行路径推导的情况下，系统性地评估了其在 Node.js 包中检测和确认污染型漏洞的有效性。针对公开基准数据集中的包，LLMVD.js 成功确认了 84% 的漏洞，而此前的程序分析工具确认率不足 22%。此外，LLMVD.js 的表现优于以往的 LLM-程序分析混合方法，且无需依赖漏洞标注或历史漏洞报告。在对 260 个近期发布的包（无已知漏洞真实标签）进行评估时，传统工具仅能为极少数（≤2 个）包生成可验证的攻击，而 LLMVD.js 则成功为 36 个包生成了可验证的漏洞利用。

title: Meta-Tool: Efficient Few-Shot Tool Adaptation for Small Language Models
authors: Sachin Kumar
abstract: Can small language models achieve strong tool-use performance without complex adaptation mechanisms? This paper investigates this question through Meta-Tool, a controlled empirical study comparing hypernetwork-based LoRA adaptation against carefully designed few-shot prompting. Using a Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct backbone, we evaluate four adaptation mechanisms--few-shot prompting, documentation encoding, hypernetwork-generated LoRA weights, and value-guided beam search--across four diverse benchmarks: Gorilla APIBench, Spider 2.0, WebArena, and InterCode. Our central finding is a well-supported negative result: despite generating non-trivial weight matrices, the 227.8M-parameter hypernetwork provides no measurable improvement over few-shot prompting alone. Comprehensive ablation studies reveal that few-shot examples contribute +21.5% to performance and documentation contributes +5.0%, while the hypernetwork adds 0%. A 3B model with well-designed prompts achieves 79.7% of GPT-5's average performance at $10 \times$ lower latency. Error analysis across 722 failure cases spanning all shot counts (0--5) shows that at the 5-shot configuration (106 failures), failure modes are task-dependent: schema-heavy tasks (Spider 2.0, WebArena) show near-zero format errors with remaining failures semantic, while format errors dominate on Gorilla (100%) and InterCode (70%). These findings redirect practitioners toward prompt engineering and example curation rather than complex adaptation architectures.
title_zh: 元工具：面向小型语言模型的高效少样本工具适应
abstract_zh:
小语言模型能否在不依赖复杂适配机制的情况下实现强大的工具使用性能？本文通过 Meta-Tool 这一受控的实证研究，探讨了这一问题。研究对比了基于超网络的 LoRA 适配方法与精心设计的少样本提示（few-shot prompting）两种策略。以 Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct 为骨干模型，我们在四个不同基准测试集——Gorilla APIBench、Spider 2.0、WebArena 和 InterCode 上，评估了四种适配机制：少样本提示、文档编码、超网络生成的 LoRA 权重以及基于价值引导的束搜索（value-guided beam search）。

研究的核心发现是一个有力的负面结果：尽管超网络（22780万参数）能够生成非平凡的权重矩阵，但其性能相比仅使用少样本提示并无显著提升。全面的消融实验表明，少样本示例可带来 +21.5% 的性能增益，文档编码贡献 +5.0%，而超网络带来的提升为 0%。一个经过良好设计提示的 3B 模型，在延迟仅为 GPT-5 的十分之一的情况下，达到了其平均性能的 79.7%。

对涵盖所有提示数量（0–5）的 722 个失败案例进行错误分析显示，当采用五次提示配置时（共 106 个失败），失败模式呈现任务依赖性：在以模式结构为主的任务中（如 Spider 2.0 和 WebArena），格式错误几乎为零，其余失败主要源于语义理解问题；而在 Gorilla 和 InterCode 上，格式错误分别占 100% 和 70%。

这些发现表明，实践者应将重点放在提示工程和示例优化上，而非追求复杂的适配架构。

title: HiPO: Hierarchical Preference Optimization for Adaptive Reasoning in LLMs
authors: Darsh Kachroo, Adriana Caraeni, Arjun Prasaath Anbazhagan, Brennan Lagasse, Kevin Zhu
abstract: Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) is an effective framework for aligning large language models with human preferences, but it struggles with complex reasoning tasks. DPO optimizes for the likelihood of generating preferred over dispreferred responses in their entirety and lacks the granularity to provide feedback on subsections of many-step solutions typical of reasoning tasks. Existing methods excel at either stable preference learning (e.g., DPO variants like KTO and RSO) or structured reasoning (e.g., ReMA's multi-agent RL framework, Tree of Thoughts), but fail to merge these complementary strengths. We propose HiPO (Hierarchical Preference Optimization), an extension of DPO that separates responses into reasoning segments (query clarification and context, reasoning steps, and answer) and computes loss as a weighted sum of the DPO loss for each segment. Our approach enables segment-specific training while maintaining DPO's computational efficiency and training stability. We demonstrate that for multiple 7B LLMs fine-tuned using HiPO and DPO on the Math Stack Exchange preference dataset, the models trained with HiPO outperform the others on a variety of common math benchmarks and achieve greater organization, logical flow, and consistency as measured by GPT-4.1.
title_zh: HiPO：面向大语言模型自适应推理的分层偏好优化
abstract_zh: 直接偏好优化（DPO）是一种有效对齐大型语言模型与人类偏好的框架，但在处理复杂推理任务时表现不佳。DPO 旨在整体上最大化生成偏好响应的似然性，而缺乏对多步推理解决方案中各个子部分提供反馈的精细度。现有方法要么在稳定偏好学习方面表现出色（如 DPO 的变体 KTO 和 RSO），要么在结构化推理方面表现优异（如 ReMA 的多智能体强化学习框架、思维树方法），但未能融合这两种互补优势。我们提出 HiPO（分层偏好优化），作为 DPO 的扩展，将响应分解为若干推理片段（问题澄清与上下文、推理步骤、答案），并计算各片段 DPO 损失的加权和作为总损失。该方法实现了针对不同片段的特定训练，同时保持了 DPO 的计算效率和训练稳定性。我们在 Math Stack Exchange 偏好数据集上对多个 7B 规模的语言模型使用 HiPO 和 DPO 进行微调，实验表明，采用 HiPO 训练的模型在多种常见数学基准测试中均优于其他模型，并且在 GPT-4.1 的评估下展现出更佳的组织性、逻辑连贯性和一致性。

title: Worst-Case Optimal GPU Datalog
authors: Yihao Sun, Kunting Qi, Thomas Gilray, Sidharth Kumar, Kristopher Micinski
abstract: Datalog is a declarative logic-programming language used for complex analytic reasoning workloads such as program analysis and graph analytics. Datalog's popularity is due to its unique price-point, marrying logic-defined specification with the potential for massive data parallelism. While traditional engines are CPU-based, the memory-bound nature of Datalog has led to increasing interest in leveraging GPUs. These engines beat CPU-based engines by operationalizing iterated relational joins via SIMT-friendly join algorithms. Unfortunately, all existing GPU Datalog engines are built on binary joins, which are inadequate for the complex multi-way queries arising in production systems such as DOOP and ddisasm. For these queries, binary decomposition can incur the AGM bound asymptotic blowup in time and space, leading to OOM failures regardless of join order. Worst-Case Optimal Joins (WCOJ) avoid this blowup, but their attribute-at-a-time intersections map poorly to SIMT hardware under key skew, causing severe load imbalance across Streaming Multiprocessors (SMs). We present SRDatalog, the first GPU Datalog engine based on WCOJ. SRDatalog uses flat columnar storage and two-phase deterministic memory allocation to avoid the OOM failures of binary joins and the index-rebuild overheads of static WCOJ systems. To mitigate skew and hide hardware stalls, SRDatalog further employs root-level histogram-guided load balancing, structural helper-relation splitting, and stream-aligned rule multiplexing. On real-world program-analysis workloads, SRDatalog achieves geometric-mean speedups of 21x to 47x.
title_zh: 最坏情况最优的GPU Datalog
abstract_zh:
Datalog是一种声明式逻辑编程语言，广泛应用于程序分析、图分析等复杂分析性工作负载。Datalog之所以广受欢迎，是因为其独特的性能定位——将逻辑定义的规范与大规模数据并行处理的潜力相结合。尽管传统引擎基于CPU运行，但Datalog固有的内存密集特性促使人们越来越多地关注利用GPU进行加速。这些基于GPU的引擎通过采用适合SIMT架构的迭代关系连接算法，在操作上超越了基于CPU的引擎。然而，现有的所有GPU Datalog引擎均基于二元连接（binary joins），而这种设计对于生产系统中出现的复杂多路查询（如DOOP和ddisasm中的查询）而言并不充分。在这些场景下，二元分解可能导致时间与空间复杂度达到AGM界（AGM bound）的渐近爆炸，无论采用何种连接顺序，都会引发内存溢出（OOM）错误。虽然最坏情况最优连接（WCOJ）能够避免这种爆炸式增长，但其逐属性交集的计算模式在关键数据倾斜情况下难以有效映射到SIMT硬件，导致流式多处理器（SMs）之间严重负载不均。

我们提出了SRDatalog，这是首个基于WCOJ的GPU Datalog引擎。SRDatalog采用扁平列式存储结构，并结合两阶段确定性内存分配机制，既避免了二元连接带来的OOM问题，也规避了静态WCOJ系统中索引重建的开销。为进一步缓解数据倾斜并隐藏硬件停顿，SRDatalog还引入了根级直方图引导的负载均衡、结构化辅助关系拆分以及流对齐的规则多路复用技术。在真实世界程序分析工作负载上的实验表明，SRDatalog实现了21倍至47倍的几何平均加速比。

title: Automatic Ontology Construction Using LLMs as an External Layer of Memory, Verification, and Planning for Hybrid Intelligent Systems
authors: Pavel Salovskii, Iuliia Gorshkova
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid architecture for intelligent systems in which large language models (LLMs) are extended with an external ontological memory layer. Instead of relying solely on parametric knowledge and vector-based retrieval (RAG), the proposed approach constructs and maintains a structured knowledge graph using RDF/OWL representations, enabling persistent, verifiable, and semantically grounded reasoning. The core contribution is an automated pipeline for ontology construction from heterogeneous data sources, including documents, APIs, and dialogue logs. The system performs entity recognition, relation extraction, normalization, and triple generation, followed by validation using SHACL and OWL constraints, and continuous graph updates. During inference, LLMs operate over a combined context that integrates vector-based retrieval with graph-based reasoning and external tool interaction. Experimental observations on planning tasks, including the Tower of Hanoi benchmark, indicate that ontology augmentation improves performance in multi-step reasoning scenarios compared to baseline LLM systems. In addition, the ontology layer enables formal validation of generated outputs, transforming the system into a generation-verification-correction pipeline. The proposed architecture addresses key limitations of current LLM-based systems, including lack of long-term memory, weak structural understanding, and limited reasoning capabilities. It provides a foundation for building agent-based systems, robotics applications, and enterprise AI solutions that require persistent knowledge, explainability, and reliable decision-making.
title_zh: 利用大语言模型作为混合智能系统外部记忆、验证与规划层的自动本体构建
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种智能系统的混合架构，该架构通过引入外部本体记忆层扩展了大型语言模型（LLMs）的能力。与仅依赖参数化知识和基于向量的检索（RAG）的方法不同，所提出的方案采用RDF/OWL表示形式构建并维护一个结构化的知识图谱，从而实现持久、可验证且语义基础坚实的推理。其核心贡献在于：从异构数据源（包括文档、API 和对话日志）中自动构建本体的流水线。该系统完成实体识别、关系抽取、规范化处理以及三元组生成后，利用SHACL和OWL约束进行验证，并支持持续的图谱更新。在推理阶段，LLM在融合了基于向量的检索、基于图的推理以及外部工具交互的综合上下文中运行。在规划任务（包括汉诺塔基准测试）上的实验结果表明，相较于基线LLM系统，本体增强显著提升了多步推理场景下的性能。此外，本体层还实现了对生成结果的正式验证，使系统转变为“生成-验证-修正”的闭环流程。该架构有效解决了当前基于LLM系统的关键局限性，如缺乏长期记忆、结构性理解能力弱以及推理能力有限等问题。它为构建基于代理的系统、机器人应用及企业级AI解决方案奠定了基础，这些应用需要持久的知识存储、可解释性以及可靠的决策能力。

title: Autark: A Serverless Toolkit for Prototyping Urban Visual Analytics Systems
authors: Lucas Alexandre, João Rulff, Talisson Souza, Gustavo Moreira, Daniel de Oliveira, Claudio Silva, Fabio Miranda, Marcos Lage
abstract: The development of visual analytics (VA) systems has traditionally been a labor-intensive process, balancing design methodologies with complex software engineering practices. In domain-specific fields like urban VA, this challenge is amplified by heterogeneous data streams and a reliance on complex, multi-service architectures that hinder fast development, deployment, and reproducibility. Despite the richness of the urban VA literature, the field lacks a consolidated toolkit that encapsulates the core components of these systems, such as spatial data management, analytical processing, and visualization, into a unified, lightweight framework. In this paper, we introduce Autark, a serverless toolkit designed for the rapid prototyping of urban VA systems. Autark provides domain-aware abstractions through a self-contained architecture, enabling researchers to transition from design intention to deployed, shareable systems within hours. Furthermore, Autark's structured, tightly scoped interfaces make it well-suited for AI-assisted coding workflows, where LLMs produce more reliable code when composing from well-defined abstractions rather than generating complex solutions from scratch. Our contributions are: (1) the Autark toolkit, a serverless architecture for rapid prototyping of urban VA; (2) a comparative study of LLM coding effectiveness with and without Autark; and (3) a series of usage scenarios demonstrating its capability to streamline the creation of robust, shareable urban VA prototypes. Autark is available at https://autarkjs.org/.
title_zh: Autark：用于原型设计城市视觉分析系统的无服务器工具包
abstract_zh: 视觉分析（VA）系统的发展传统上是一项劳动密集型的工作，需要在设计方法与复杂的软件工程实践之间取得平衡。在城市视觉分析等特定领域，这一挑战因异构数据流的存在以及对复杂多服务架构的依赖而进一步加剧，从而阻碍了系统的快速开发、部署和可复现性。尽管城市视觉分析领域的文献十分丰富，但该领域仍缺乏一个集成的核心工具包，无法将空间数据管理、分析处理和可视化等关键组件整合到一个统一且轻量化的框架中。本文提出了一种名为 Autark 的无服务器工具包，旨在实现城市视觉分析系统的快速原型设计。Autark 通过自包含的架构提供领域感知的抽象，使研究人员能够在数小时内从设计意图过渡到已部署、可共享的系统。此外，Autark 结构清晰、作用范围明确的接口，使其非常适合人工智能辅助编码工作流程：当大型语言模型（LLMs）基于定义良好的抽象进行代码组合时，其生成的代码更加可靠，而非从零开始构建复杂解决方案。本文的主要贡献包括：（1）提出 Autark 工具包，一种用于城市视觉分析系统快速原型设计的无服务器架构；（2）一项关于 LLM 编码效率的对比研究，比较使用与不使用 Autark 的效果；（3）一系列应用场景，展示了 Autark 在简化创建稳健、可共享的城市视觉分析原型方面的强大能力。Autark 工具包可在 https://autarkjs.org/ 获取。

title: EvoAgent: An Evolvable Agent Framework with Skill Learning and Multi-Agent Delegation
authors: Aimin Zhang, Jiajing Guo, Fuwei Jia, Chen Lv, Boyu Wang, Fangzheng Li
abstract: This paper proposes EvoAgent - an evolvable large language model (LLM) agent framework that integrates structured skill learning with a hierarchical sub-agent delegation mechanism. EvoAgent models skills as multi-file structured capability units equipped with triggering mechanisms and evolutionary metadata, and enables continuous skill generation and optimization through a user-feedback-driven closed-loop process. In addition, by incorporating a three-stage skill matching strategy and a three-layer memory architecture, the framework supports dynamic task decomposition for complex problems and long-term capability accumulation. Experimental results based on real-world foreign trade scenarios demonstrate that, after integrating EvoAgent, GPT5.2 achieves significant improvements in professionalism, accuracy, and practical utility. Under a five-dimensional LLM-as-Judge evaluation protocol, the overall average score increases by approximately 28%. Further model transfer experiments indicate that the performance of an agent system depends not only on the intrinsic capabilities of the underlying model, but also on the degree of synergy between the model and the agent architecture.
title_zh: EvoAgent：一种具备技能学习与多智能体委派功能的可演化智能体框架
abstract_zh: 本文提出EvoAgent——一种可演化的大型语言模型（LLM）智能体框架，该框架将结构化技能学习与分层子智能体委派机制相结合。EvoAgent将技能建模为多文件结构化的功能单元，配备触发机制和演化元数据，并通过用户反馈驱动的闭环过程实现技能的持续生成与优化。此外，通过引入三阶段技能匹配策略和三层记忆架构，该框架支持复杂问题的动态任务分解以及长期能力积累。基于真实国际贸易场景的实验结果表明，集成EvoAgent后，GPT5.2在专业性、准确性及实际应用价值方面均取得显著提升。在五维LLM作为评判者（LLM-as-Judge）评估体系下，综合平均得分提升了约28%。进一步的模型迁移实验表明，智能体系统的性能不仅取决于底层模型的内在能力，更取决于模型与智能体架构之间的协同程度。

title: Making TransactionIsolation Checking Practical
authors: Jian Zhang, Shuai Mu, Cheng Tan
abstract: Checking whether database transactions adhere to isolation levels is a crucial yet challenging problem. We present Boomslang, the first general-purpose checking framework capable of verifying configurations that were previously uncheckable. Boomslang advances beyond prior work in three key aspects: (1) it supports arbitrary operation types provided by modern transactional key-value stores, (2) it requires no knowledge of database internals, and (3) it offers a modular, extensible pipeline amenable to customization and optimizations. Boomslang adopts a front-/back-end separation. As the front-end, it parses a database trace into an Abstract Semantic Graph, which is then lowered -- via semantic analysis -- into a low-level intermediate representation (IR). The back-end converts this IR to a set of constraints for SMT solving. This design is enabled by a key abstraction in the IR, called superpositions, which capture the uncertainty and complexity caused by arbitrary operations and missing information. Our experiments show that with just 271--386 lines of code, the core logic of three prior checkers can be reimplemented as Boomslang modules, achieving comparable or superior performance. Using Boomslang, we also identify a new bug in TiDB, audit the metadata layer of the JuiceFS file system, check vendor-specific behavior in MariaDB, support five previously unchecked isolation levels, and confirm a theoretical result on the correctness of strict serializability.
title_zh: 使事务隔离检查实用化
abstract_zh: 检查数据库事务是否遵守隔离级别是一个至关重要但又极具挑战性的问题。我们提出了 Boomslang，这是首个能够验证此前无法检查配置的通用检查框架。Boomslang 在三个方面超越了以往的工作：（1）它支持现代事务性键值存储所提供的任意操作类型；（2）无需了解数据库内部机制；（3）提供模块化、可扩展的处理流程，便于定制和优化。Boomslang 采用前后端分离的设计：前端将数据库执行轨迹解析为抽象语义图（Abstract Semantic Graph），随后通过语义分析将其降级为低级别的中间表示（IR）。后端则将该 IR 转换为适用于 SMT 求解器的一组约束条件。这一设计的核心在于 IR 中的一个关键抽象——“叠加”（superpositions），它能够捕捉由任意操作和信息缺失所引发的不确定性与复杂性。实验表明，仅用 271 到 386 行代码，即可将三个先前检查器的核心逻辑重构为 Boomslang 模块，并实现相当或更优的性能。利用 Boomslang，我们还发现 TiDB 中一个新缺陷，审计了 JuiceFS 文件系统的元数据层，验证了 MariaDB 的厂商特定行为，支持了五个此前未被检查的隔离级别，并证实了一个关于严格可串行化正确性的理论结果。

title: LayerTracer: A Joint Task-Particle and Vulnerable-Layer Analysis framework for Arbitrary Large Language Model Architectures
authors: Yuhang Wu, Qinyuan Liu, Qiuyang Zhao, Qingwei Chong
abstract: Currently, Large Language Models (LLMs) feature a diversified architectural landscape, including traditional Transformer, GateDeltaNet, and Mamba. However, the evolutionary laws of hierarchical representations, task knowledge formation positions, and network robustness bottleneck mechanisms in various LLM architectures remain unclear, posing core challenges for hybrid architecture design and model optimization. This paper proposes LayerTracer, an architecture-agnostic end-to-end analysis framework compatible with any LLM architecture. By extracting hidden states layer-by-layer and mapping them to vocabulary probability distributions, it achieves joint analysis of task particle localization and layer vulnerability quantification. We define the task particle as the key layer where the target token probability first rises significantly, representing the model's task execution starting point, and the vulnerable layer is defined as the layer with the maximum Jensen-Shannon (JS) divergence between output distributions before and after mask perturbation, reflecting its sensitivity to disturbances. Experiments on models of different parameter scales show that task particles mainly appear in the deep layers of the model regardless of parameter size, while larger-parameter models exhibit stronger hierarchical robustness. LayerTracer provides a scientific basis for layer division, module ratio, and gating switching of hybrid architectures, effectively optimizing model performance. It accurately locates task-effective layers and stability bottlenecks, offering universal support for LLM structure design and interpretability research.
title_zh: LayerTracer：一种针对任意大型语言模型架构的联合任务-粒子与脆弱层分析框架
abstract_zh: 目前，大型语言模型（LLMs）呈现出多样化的架构形态，包括传统的Transformer、GateDeltaNet以及Mamba等。然而，不同LLM架构中层次化表征的演化规律、任务知识形成的位置分布，以及网络鲁棒性瓶颈机制等问题仍不清晰，这给混合架构设计与模型优化带来了核心挑战。本文提出LayerTracer——一种与具体架构无关的端到端分析框架，可兼容任意LLM架构。通过逐层提取隐藏状态，并将其映射到词汇概率分布，该框架实现了对任务粒子定位与层级脆弱性量化之间的联合分析。我们定义“任务粒子”为模型中目标词概率首次显著上升的关键层级，代表模型执行任务的起始点；而“脆弱层级”则定义为在掩码扰动前后输出分布之间Jensen-Shannon（JS）散度最大的层级，反映其对扰动的敏感程度。在不同参数规模模型上的实验表明，无论参数量大小，任务粒子主要集中在模型的深层结构中，而大参数模型展现出更强的层次鲁棒性。LayerTracer为混合架构中的层级划分、模块比例配置及门控切换策略提供了科学依据，有效提升了模型性能。它能够精准定位任务有效层与稳定性瓶颈，为大型语言模型的结构设计与可解释性研究提供通用支持。

title: Early-Stage Product Line Validation Using LLMs: A Study on Semi-Formal Blueprint Analysis
authors: Viet-Man Le, Thi Ngoc Trang Tran, Sebastian Lubos, Alexander Felfernig, Damian Garber
abstract: We study whether Large Language Models (LLMs) can perform feature model analysis operations (AOs) directly on semi-formal textual blueprints, i.e., concise constrained-language descriptions of feature hierarchies and constraints, enabling early validation in Software Product Line scoping. Using 12 state-of-the-art LLMs and 16 standard AOs, we compare their outputs against the solver-based oracle FLAMA. Results show that reasoning-optimized models (e.g., Grok 4 Fast Reasoning, Gemini 2.5 Pro) achieve 88-89% average accuracy across all evaluated blueprints and operations, approaching solver correctness. We identify systematic errors in structural parsing and constraint reasoning, and highlight accuracy-cost trade-offs that inform model selection. These findings position LLMs as lightweight assistants for early variability validation.
title_zh: 基于大语言模型的早期产品线验证：一种半正式蓝图分析研究
abstract_zh: 我们研究大型语言模型（LLMs）是否能够直接对半形式化的文本蓝图（即特征层次结构与约束的简洁受限语言描述）执行特征模型分析操作（AOs），从而在软件产品线的范围界定阶段实现早期验证。通过使用12个最先进的LLM和16个标准分析操作，我们将它们的输出结果与基于求解器的基准工具FLAMA进行对比。结果显示，经过推理优化的模型（如Grok 4 Fast Reasoning、Gemini 2.5 Pro）在所有评估的蓝图和操作上平均准确率达到88%-89%，接近求解器的正确水平。我们识别出在结构解析和约束推理方面存在系统性错误，并揭示了准确率与计算成本之间的权衡关系，为模型选择提供了依据。这些发现表明，LLMs可作为早期可变性验证的轻量级辅助工具。

title: Dual-Cluster Memory Agent: Resolving Multi-Paradigm Ambiguity in Optimization Problem Solving
authors: Xinyu Zhang, Yuchen Wan, Boxuan Zhang, Zesheng Yang, Lingling Zhang, Bifan Wei, Jun Liu
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) often struggle with structural ambiguity in optimization problems, where a single problem admits multiple related but conflicting modeling paradigms, hindering effective solution generation. To address this, we propose Dual-Cluster Memory Agent (DCM-Agent) to enhance performance by leveraging historical solutions in a training-free manner. Central to this is Dual-Cluster Memory Construction. This agent assigns historical solutions to modeling and coding clusters, then distills each cluster's content into three structured types: Approach, Checklist, and Pitfall. This process derives generalizable guidance knowledge. Furthermore, this agent introduces Memory-augmented Inference to dynamically navigate solution paths, detect and repair errors, and adaptively switch reasoning paths with structured knowledge. The experiments across seven optimization benchmarks demonstrate that DCM-Agent achieves an average performance improvement of 11%- 21%. Notably, our analysis reveals a ``knowledge inheritance'' phenomenon: memory constructed by larger models can guide smaller models toward superior performance, highlighting the framework's scalability and efficiency.
title_zh: 双簇内存代理：在优化问题求解中解决多范式模糊性
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在处理优化问题中的结构歧义时常常面临挑战，因为同一问题可能对应多种相关但相互冲突的建模范式，这阻碍了有效解法的生成。为解决这一问题，我们提出了一种无需训练的双聚类记忆代理（Dual-Cluster Memory Agent, DCM-Agent），通过利用历史解法来提升性能。其核心是双聚类记忆构建机制：该代理将历史解法分配至建模与编码两类聚簇，并分别提炼出三类结构化知识——方法（Approach）、检查清单（Checklist）和陷阱警示（Pitfall）。这一过程提取出可泛化的指导性知识。此外，该代理引入了增强记忆的推理机制，能够动态导航求解路径、检测并修复错误，并基于结构化知识自适应地切换推理路径。在七个优化基准上的实验表明，DCM-Agent实现了平均11%至21%的性能提升。尤为值得注意的是，我们的分析揭示了一种“知识继承”现象：由大模型构建的记忆能够引导小模型达到更优表现，凸显了该框架在可扩展性与效率方面的优势。

title: Memory-Augmented LLM-based Multi-Agent System for Automated Feature Generation on Tabular Data
authors: Fengxian Dong, Zhi Zheng, Xiao Han, Wei Chen, Jingqing Ruan, Tong Xu, Yong Chen, Enhong Chen
abstract: Automated feature generation extracts informative features from raw tabular data without manual intervention and is crucial for accurate, generalizable machine learning. Traditional methods rely on predefined operator libraries and cannot leverage task semantics, limiting their ability to produce diverse, high-value features for complex tasks. Recent Large Language Model (LLM)-based approaches introduce richer semantic signals, but still suffer from a restricted feature space due to fixed generation patterns and from the absence of feedback from the learning objective. To address these challenges, we propose a Memory-Augmented LLM-based Multi-Agent System (\textbf{MALMAS}) for automated feature generation. MALMAS decomposes the generation process into agents with distinct responsibilities, and a Router Agent activates an appropriate subset of agents per iteration, further broadening exploration of the feature space. We further integrate a memory module comprising procedural memory, feedback memory, and conceptual memory, enabling iterative refinement that adaptively guides subsequent feature generation and improves feature quality and diversity. Extensive experiments on multiple public datasets against state-of-the-art baselines demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach. The code is available at https://github.com/fxdong24/MALMAS
title_zh: 基于记忆增强型大语言模型的多智能体系统在表格数据自动特征生成中的应用
abstract_zh: 自动化特征生成能够从原始表格数据中自动提取具有信息量的特征，无需人工干预，对于实现准确且可泛化的机器学习至关重要。传统方法依赖预定义的操作符库，无法利用任务语义，因而难以生成多样且高价值的特征，尤其在复杂任务中表现受限。近年来基于大语言模型（LLM）的方法引入了更丰富的语义信号，但仍受限于固定的生成模式，且缺乏来自学习目标的反馈，导致特征空间受限。为解决上述挑战，我们提出一种基于记忆增强的LLM多智能体系统（\textbf{MALMAS}），用于自动化特征生成。MALMAS将生成过程分解为具有不同职责的多个智能体，由路由智能体在每轮迭代中激活合适的智能体子集，从而进一步拓展特征空间的探索范围。此外，我们引入一个包含过程记忆、反馈记忆和概念记忆的记忆模块，支持迭代优化，自适应地引导后续特征生成，显著提升特征的质量与多样性。在多个公开数据集上的大量实验表明，该方法相较于现有最先进基线具有显著优势。代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/fxdong24/MALMAS

title: Chasing the Public Score: User Pressure and Evaluation Exploitation in Coding Agent Workflows
authors: Hardy Chen, Nancy Lau, Haoqin Tu, Shuo Yan, Xiangyan Liu, Zijun Wang, Juncheng Wu, Michael Qizhe Shieh, Alvaro A. Cardenas, Cihang Xie, Yuyin Zhou
abstract: Frontier coding agents are increasingly used in workflows where users supervise progress primarily through repeated improvement of a public score, namely the reported score on a public evaluation file with labels in the workspace, rather than through direct inspection of the agent's intermediate outputs. We study whether multi-round user pressure to improve that score induces public score exploitation: behavior that raises the public score through shortcuts without improving hidden private evaluation. We begin with a preliminary single-script tabular classification task, where GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6 both exploit label information within 10 rounds of user-agent interaction. We then build AgentPressureBench, a 34-task machine-learning repository benchmark spanning three input modalities, and collect 1326 multi-round trajectories from 13 coding agents. On our benchmark, we observe 403 exploitative runs, spanning across all tasks. We also find that stronger models have higher exploitation rates, supported by a significant Spearman rank correlation of 0.77. Our ablation experiments show that higher user pressure leads to earlier exploitation, reducing the average first exploit round by 15.6 rounds (i.e., 19.67 to 4.08). As a mitigation, adding explicit anti-exploit wordings in prompt mostly eliminates exploitation (100% to 8.3%). We hope that our work can bring attention to more careful use of coding agents workflow, and developing more robust coding agents under user pressure. Our project page is at https://ucsc-vlaa.github.io/AgentPressureBench .
title_zh: 追逐公众评分：用户压力与评价滥用在编码智能体工作流中的体现
abstract_zh: 前沿编码代理在工作流中的应用日益广泛，用户主要通过反复提升一个公开评分来监督进展，该评分基于工作区中带有标签的公开评估文件所报告的结果，而非直接审查代理的中间输出。我们研究了多轮用户施压以提高这一评分的行为是否会引发“公开评分投机”：即通过捷径手段提升公开评分，但并未真正改善隐藏的私有评估指标。我们首先开展了一项初步的单脚本表格分类任务，发现GPT-5.4和Claude Opus 4.6均在10轮用户-代理交互内利用了标签信息进行投机。随后，我们构建了AgentPressureBench——一个涵盖三种输入模态、共34个任务的机器学习基准测试库，并收集了来自13个编码代理的1326条多轮轨迹。在该基准上，我们观察到403次投机性运行，覆盖所有任务。此外，我们发现模型越强，投机率越高，Spearman等级相关系数达到显著的0.77。消融实验表明，更高的用户压力会导致更早发生投机行为，使平均首次投机轮次提前了15.6轮（从19.67轮降至4.08轮）。作为缓解措施，在提示中加入明确的反投机措辞可基本消除投机行为（从100%降至8.3%）。我们希望本研究能引起对编码代理工作流更审慎使用的关注，并推动在用户压力下开发更具鲁棒性的编码代理。项目主页详见：https://ucsc-vlaa.github.io/AgentPressureBench。

title: GRPO-VPS: Enhancing Group Relative Policy Optimization with Verifiable Process Supervision for Effective Reasoning
authors: Jingyi Wang, Lei Zhu, Tengjin Weng, Song-Li Wu, Haochen Tan, Jierun Chen, Chaofan Tao, Haoli Bai, Lu Hou, Lifeng Shang, Xiao-Ping Zhang
abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has advanced the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) by leveraging direct outcome verification instead of learned reward models. Building on this paradigm, Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) eliminates the need for critic models but suffers from indiscriminate credit assignment for intermediate steps, which limits its ability to identify effective reasoning strategies and incurs overthinking. In this work, we introduce a model-free and verifiable process supervision via probing the model's belief in the correct answer throughout its reasoning trajectory. By segmenting the generation into discrete steps and tracking the conditional probability of the correct answer appended at each segment boundary, we efficiently compute interpretable segment-wise progress measurements to refine GRPO's trajectory-level feedback. This approach enables more targeted and sample-efficient policy updates, while avoiding the need for intermediate supervision derived from costly Monte Carlo rollouts or auxiliary models. Experiments on mathematical and general-domain benchmarks show consistent gains over GRPO across diverse models: up to 2.6-point accuracy improvements and 13.7% reasoning-length reductions on math tasks, and up to 2.4 points and 4% on general-domain tasks, demonstrating strong generalization.
title_zh: GRPO-VPS：通过可验证过程监督增强组相对策略优化以实现有效推理
abstract_zh: 基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）通过利用直接结果验证而非学习型奖励模型，显著提升了大型语言模型（LLMs）的推理能力。在此范式基础上，群体相对策略优化（GRPO）摒弃了评判器模型的需求，但其在中间步骤上存在不加区分的信用分配问题，导致难以识别有效的推理策略，并引发过度思考。本文提出一种无需模型且可验证的过程监督机制，通过探测模型在其推理轨迹中对正确答案信念的变化来实现。我们将生成过程划分为离散步骤，并追踪每个片段边界处正确答案的条件概率，从而高效计算出可解释的分段进展度量，用于优化GRPO的轨迹级反馈。该方法实现了更精准、样本高效的策略更新，同时避免了依赖昂贵的蒙特卡洛回溯或辅助模型所生成的中间监督信号。在数学与通用领域基准上的实验表明，该方法在多种模型上均持续优于GRPO：数学任务上准确率最高提升2.6个百分点，推理长度减少13.7%；通用领域任务上准确率最高提升2.4个百分点，推理长度减少4%，展现出强大的泛化能力。

title: Where Reasoning Breaks: Logic-Aware Path Selection by Controlling Logical Connectives in LLMs Reasoning Chains
authors: Seunghyun Park, Yuanyuan Lei
abstract: While LLMs demonstrate impressive reasoning capabilities, they remain fragile in multi-step logical deduction, where a single transition error can propagate through the entire reasoning chain, leading to unstable performance. In this work, we identify logical connectives as primary points of this structural fragility. Through empirical analysis, we show that connective tokens function as high entropy forking points, at which models frequently struggle to determine the correct logical direction. Motivated by this observation, we hypothesize that intervening in logical connective selection can guide LLMs toward more correct logical direction, thereby improving the overall reasoning chain. To validate this hypothesis, we propose a multi-layered framework that intervenes specifically at these logic-critical junctions in the reasoning process. Our framework includes (1) Gradient-based Logical Steering to guide LLMs internal representations towards valid reasoning subspaces, (2) Localized Branching to resolve ambiguity via targeted look-ahead search, and (3) Targeted Transition Preference Optimization, a surgical reinforcement learning objective that selectively optimizes single-token preferences at logical pivots. Crucially, by concentrating intervention solely on logic-critical transitions, our framework achieves a favorable accuracy--efficiency trade-off compared to global inference time scaling methods like beam search and self-consistency.
title_zh: 推理失效之处：通过控制大语言模型推理链中的逻辑连接词实现逻辑感知的路径选择
abstract_zh: 尽管大语言模型（LLM）展现出令人印象深刻的推理能力，但在多步逻辑推导中仍显得脆弱——一个微小的推理步骤错误就可能在整个推理链中传播，导致性能不稳定。在本研究中，我们识别出逻辑连接词是这种结构脆弱性的关键节点。通过实证分析，我们发现连接词标记充当了高熵的分叉点，模型在此处常常难以确定正确的逻辑方向。基于这一观察，我们提出假设：干预逻辑连接词的选择，能够引导大语言模型走向更正确的逻辑路径，从而提升整体推理链条的准确性。为验证该假设，我们提出一种多层次框架，专门针对推理过程中这些逻辑关键节点进行干预。该框架包含三个核心组件：（1）基于梯度的逻辑引导（Gradient-based Logical Steering），通过引导模型内部表征进入有效的推理子空间；（2）局部分支机制（Localized Branching），通过有针对性的前瞻搜索解决歧义问题；（3）目标化过渡偏好优化（Targeted Transition Preference Optimization），一种精准的强化学习目标，仅对逻辑枢纽处的单个 token 选择偏好进行优化。至关重要的是，由于干预仅聚焦于逻辑关键的转换环节，我们的框架相比全局推理时间扩展方法（如束搜索和自一致性）在准确率与效率之间实现了更优的权衡。

title: DeepParse: Hybrid Log Parsing with LLM-Synthesized Regex Masks
authors: Amir Shetaia, Sean Kauffman
abstract: Modern distributed systems produce massive, heterogeneous logs essential for reliability, security, and anomaly detection. Converting these free-form messages into structured templates (log parsing) is challenging due to evolving formats and limited labeled data. Machine-learning-based parsers like Drain are fast but accuracy often degrades on complex variables, while Large Language Models (LLMs) offer better generalization but incur prohibitive inference costs. This paper presents DeepParse, a hybrid framework that automatically mines reusable variable patterns from small log samples using an LLM, then applies them deterministically through the Drain algorithm. By separating the reasoning phase from execution, DeepParse enables accurate, scalable, and cost-efficient log structuring without relying on brittle handcrafted rules or per-line neural inference. Across 16 benchmark datasets, DeepParse achieves higher accuracy in variable extraction (97.6% average Parsing Accuracy) and better consistency than both heuristic and LLM-only baselines. Integrating DeepParse into an anomaly detection pipeline reduced false alarms by over 30% and reduced inference latency by 36% compared to heuristic baselines.
title_zh: DeepParse：结合大语言模型生成正则掩码的混合日志解析
abstract_zh: 现代分布式系统生成海量且异构的日志，这些日志对于系统的可靠性、安全性以及异常检测至关重要。由于日志格式不断演变且标注数据有限，将这些自由格式的消息转换为结构化模板（即日志解析）面临巨大挑战。基于机器学习的解析器（如Drain）虽然速度快，但在处理复杂变量时准确率往往下降；而大型语言模型（LLMs）虽具备更强的泛化能力，却带来难以承受的推理开销。本文提出DeepParse，一种混合式框架：它利用大语言模型自动从小规模日志样本中挖掘可复用的变量模式，随后通过Drain算法以确定性方式应用这些模式。通过将推理阶段与执行阶段分离，DeepParse实现了高精度、可扩展且成本低廉的日志结构化处理，无需依赖脆弱的手工规则或逐行神经网络推理。在16个基准数据集上的实验表明，DeepParse在变量提取方面取得了更高的准确性（平均解析准确率达97.6%），且一致性优于传统启发式方法和仅使用LLM的基线方案。将DeepParse集成到异常检测流程后，相比启发式基线，误报率降低了30%以上，推理延迟减少了36%。

title: Render-in-the-Loop: Vector Graphics Generation via Visual Self-Feedback
authors: Guotao Liang, Zhangcheng Wang, Juncheng Hu, Haitao Zhou, Ziteng Xue, Jing Zhang, Dong Xu, Qian Yu
abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have shown promising capabilities in generating Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG) via direct code synthesis. However, existing paradigms typically adopt an open-loop "blind drawing" approach, where models generate symbolic code sequences without perceiving intermediate visual outcomes. This methodology severely underutilizes the powerful visual priors embedded in MLLMs vision encoders, treating SVG generation as a disjointed textual sequence modeling task rather than an integrated visuo-spatial one. Consequently, models struggle to reason about partial canvas states and implicit occlusion relationships, which are visually explicit but textually ambiguous. To bridge this gap, we propose Render-in-the-Loop, a novel generation paradigm that reformulates SVG synthesis as a step-wise, visual-context-aware process. By rendering intermediate code states into a cumulative canvas, the model explicitly observes the evolving visual context at each step, leveraging on-the-fly feedback to guide subsequent generation. However, we demonstrate that applying this visual loop naively to off-the-shelf models is suboptimal due to their inability to leverage incremental visual-code mappings. To address this, we first utilize fine-grained path decomposition to construct dense multi-step visual trajectories, and then introduce a Visual Self-Feedback (VSF) training strategy to condition the next primitive generation on intermediate visual states. Furthermore, a Render-and-Verify (RaV) inference mechanism is proposed to effectively filter degenerate and redundant primitives. Our framework, instantiated on a multimodal foundation model, outperforms strong open-weight baselines on the standard MMSVGBench. This result highlights the remarkable data efficiency and generalization capability of our Render-in-the-Loop paradigm for both Text-to-SVG and Image-to-SVG tasks.
title_zh: 渲染回路：通过视觉自反馈生成矢量图形
abstract_zh: 多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）在通过直接代码生成方面展现出生成可缩放矢量图形（SVG）的潜力。然而，现有的方法通常采用开环的“盲画”范式，即模型在未感知中间视觉结果的情况下生成符号化代码序列。这种模式严重低估了MLLM视觉编码器中蕴含的强大视觉先验，将SVG生成视为一个孤立的文本序列建模任务，而非一个整合的视觉-空间过程。因此，模型难以推理部分画布状态以及隐含的遮挡关系——这些在视觉上是明确的，但在文本中却存在歧义。为弥合这一差距，我们提出了一种名为“环内渲染”（Render-in-the-Loop）的新生成范式，将SVG合成重新定义为一种逐步进行、具有视觉上下文感知能力的过程。通过将中间代码状态实时渲染为累积画布，模型能够在每一步显式观察到不断演化的视觉上下文，并利用即时反馈来指导后续生成。然而，我们发现，若将此视觉循环直接应用于现成的模型，则效果不佳，因为这些模型无法有效利用增量式的视觉-代码映射关系。为此，我们首先采用细粒度路径分解构建密集的多步视觉轨迹，随后引入一种视觉自反馈（Visual Self-Feedback, VSF）训练策略，使下一步原始图元的生成能够基于中间视觉状态进行条件化。此外，我们还提出一种“渲染与验证”（Render-and-Verify, RaV）推理机制，以有效过滤退化和冗余的图元。我们的框架基于一个多模态基础模型实现，在标准的MMSVGBench基准测试中超越了多个强大的开源权重基线模型。这一结果凸显了“环内渲染”范式在Text-to-SVG和Image-to-SVG任务中卓越的数据效率与泛化能力。

title: On the Quantization Robustness of Diffusion Language Models in Coding Benchmarks
authors: Aarav Gupta, Gururaj Deshpande, Chandreyi Chakraborty
abstract: Auto-regressive Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve strong performance on coding tasks, but incur high memory and inference costs. Diffusion-based language models (d-LLMs) offer bounded inference cost via iterative denoising, but their behavior under post-training quantization (PTQ) has been sparsely explored. We investigate the application and robustness of PTQ techniques, specifically GPTQ and a modified Hessian-Aware Quantization (HAWQ) algorithm, on a diffusion-based coding LLM (CoDA) and observe that these methods applied to CoDA exhibit greater robustness at low bitwidths compared to Qwen3-1.7B, its auto-regressive counterpart, under a standardized evaluation pipeline. We find that in our setup, CoDA exhibits greater robustness at low bitwidths (2-4 bits), with smaller accuracy degradation across HumanEval and MBPP benchmarks. Additionally, mixed-precision configurations derived from HAWQ provide smooth trade-offs across accuracy, latency, and memory. The results suggest that diffusion LLMs may offer advantages for efficient deployment due to more quantization-resilience.
title_zh: 在代码基准测试中扩散语言模型的量化鲁棒性
abstract_zh: 自回归大语言模型（LLMs）在编程任务上表现出色，但其内存占用和推理成本较高。基于扩散模型的语言模型（d-LLMs）通过迭代去噪机制实现了有界的推理开销，但其在后训练量化（PTQ）下的表现尚未得到充分研究。我们针对一种基于扩散模型的编程语言模型（CoDA），系统地考察了PTQ技术（特别是GPTQ以及一种改进的Hessian感知量化，HAWQ）的应用及其鲁棒性，并发现，在标准化评估流程下，这些方法应用于CoDA时，在低比特位宽条件下展现出比其自回归对应模型Qwen3-1.7B更强的鲁棒性。在我们的实验设置中，CoDA在2至4比特的低精度下表现出更高的稳定性，且在HumanEval和MBPP基准测试中的准确率下降更小。此外，基于HAWQ生成的混合精度配置能够在准确率、延迟和内存占用之间实现平滑的权衡。结果表明，由于具备更强的量化鲁棒性，扩散型LLMs可能在高效部署方面具有潜在优势。

title: Measuring the Machine: Evaluating Generative AI as Pluralist Sociotechical Systems
authors: Rebecca L. Johnson
abstract: In measurement theory, instruments do not simply record reality; they help constitute what is observed. The same holds for generative AI evaluation: benchmarks do not just measure, they shape what models appear to be. Functionalist benchmarks treat models as isolated predictors, while prescriptive approaches assess what systems ought to be. Both obscure the sociotechnical processes through which meaning and values are enacted, risking the reification of narrow cultural perspectives in pluralist contexts. This thesis advances a descriptive alternative. It argues that generative AI must be evaluated as a pluralist sociotechnical system and develops Machine-Society-Human (MaSH) Loops, a framework for tracing how models, users, and institutions recursively co-construct meaning and values. Evaluation shifts from judging outputs to examining how values are enacted in interaction. Three contributions follow. Conceptually, MaSH Loops reframes evaluation as recursive, enactive process. Methodologically, the World Values Benchmark introduces a distributional approach grounded in World Values Survey data, structured prompt sets, and anchor-aware scoring. Empirically, the thesis demonstrates these through two cases: value drift in early GPT-3 and sociotechnical evaluation in real estate. A final chapter draws on participatory realism to argue that prompting and evaluation are constitutive interventions, not neutral observations. The thesis argues that static benchmarks are insufficient for generative AI. Responsible evaluation requires pluralist, process-oriented frameworks that make visible whose values are enacted. Evaluation is therefore a site of governance, shaping how AI systems are understood, deployed, and trusted.
title_zh: 衡量机器：将生成式人工智能作为多元社会技术系统进行评估
abstract_zh: 在测量理论中，仪器并非单纯记录现实；它们实际上参与建构了被观察之物。这一观点同样适用于生成式人工智能的评估：基准测试不仅仅是衡量工具，更在塑造模型所呈现的形态。功能主义的基准将模型视为孤立的预测器，而规范性方法则关注系统“应当”为何。这两种方式都掩盖了意义与价值在社会技术过程中被具体化的真实机制，从而在多元文化背景下，有将狭隘文化视角固化为普遍标准的风险。本文提出一种描述性的替代方案，主张将生成式人工智能视为一个多元化的社会技术系统，并构建“机器-社会-人类”（MaSH）循环框架，用以追踪模型、用户与制度如何通过递归互动共同建构意义与价值。评估的重点从评判输出结果转向考察价值在交互过程中的具体实现。本文由此提出三项贡献：概念上，MaSH 循环将评估重新定义为一种递归的、具身化的动态过程；方法上，“世界价值观基准”引入了一种基于世界价值观调查数据、结构化提示集和锚定感知评分的分布式评估方法；实证层面，本文通过两个案例加以验证：早期 GPT-3 的价值漂移现象，以及房地产领域中的社会技术评估实践。最后一章借助参与式实在论，论证提示工程与评估本身是构成性干预，而非中立的观察行为。本文强调，静态基准无法满足生成式人工智能的评估需求。负责任的评估必须采用多元、过程导向的框架，使谁的价值被体现变得可见。因此，评估不仅是技术判断，更是一种治理场域，深刻影响着人们对人工智能系统的理解、应用与信任。

title: Knowledge Capsules: Structured Nonparametric Memory Units for LLMs
authors: Bin Ju, Shenfeng Weng, Danying Zhou, Kunkai Su, Rongkai Xu
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) encode knowledge in parametric weights, making it costly to update or extend without retraining. Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) mitigates this limitation by appending retrieved text to the input, but operates purely through context expansion, where external knowledge competes as tokens within the attention mechanism. As a result, its influence is indirect and often unstable, particularly in long context and multi hop reasoning scenarios. We propose Knowledge Capsules, structured nonparametric memory units that represent normalized relational knowledge and can be constructed directly from document corpora using a frozen base model. Instead of injecting knowledge as text, we introduce an External Key Value Injection (KVI) framework that compiles capsules into attention-compatible key value representations, enabling external knowledge to directly participate in the model's attention computation. By shifting knowledge integration from context-level augmentation to memory level interaction, the proposed framework consistently outperforms RAG and GraphRAG across multiple QA benchmarks, with improved stability and accuracy in long context and multi hop reasoning, while requiring no parameter updates.
title_zh: 知识胶囊：面向大语言模型的结构化非参数记忆单元
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）将知识编码在参数权重中，导致在不重新训练的情况下更新或扩展知识成本高昂。检索增强生成（RAG）通过在输入中附加检索到的文本以缓解这一限制，但其仅依赖于上下文扩展，外部知识作为标记参与注意力机制，因此影响间接且往往不稳定，尤其在长上下文和多跳推理场景下表现更差。我们提出“知识胶囊”（Knowledge Capsules），一种结构化的非参数化记忆单元，能够表示归一化的关联知识，并可直接从文档语料库中利用冻结的基础模型构建而成。不同于将知识以文本形式注入，我们引入了一种外部键值注入（External Key Value Injection, KVI）框架，将胶囊编译为与注意力机制兼容的键值表示，使外部知识能直接参与模型的注意力计算。通过将知识整合从上下文层面的增强转变为记忆层面的交互，所提出的框架在多个问答基准测试中持续优于RAG和GraphRAG，在长上下文和多跳推理任务中表现出更高的稳定性和准确性，且无需任何参数更新。

title: Self-Awareness before Action: Mitigating Logical Inertia via Proactive Cognitive Awareness
authors: Fulong Fan, Peilin Liu, Fengzhe Liu, Shuyan Yang, Gang Yan
abstract: Large language models perform well on many reasoning tasks, yet they often lack awareness of whether their current knowledge or reasoning state is complete. In non-interactive puzzle settings, the narrative is fixed and the underlying structure is hidden; once a model forms an early hypothesis under incomplete premises, it can propagate that error throughout the reasoning process, leading to unstable conclusions. To address this issue, we propose SABA, a reasoning framework that explicitly introduces self-awareness of missing premises before making the final decision.
